australia.title: Emotions run high at inquiry into Victorian bushfires
summary: Summarize this news article:
A parliamentary inquiry into the devastating bushfires across Victoria over the summer has heard the emotional plea of those impacted in the blazes.
Fifty-four homes and businesses were destroyed by the fire, many of them in Harcourt, north-east of Castlemaine in central Victoria.
The parliamentary inquiry is being held at the Harcourt Leisure Centre, which became a community-run relief centre in the weeks after the blaze.
Representatives from Mount Alexander Shire Council, the Harcourt Progress Association, the Victorian Farmers Federation, Wildlife Victoria and DJAARA are among those making submissions this week.
Coolstore Cooperative manager Trevor Peeler told the inquiry he spent "all night" defending his home from the blaze, only to find his business destroyed the next day.
"It was completely devastated, the whole building was вҖҰ you could tell there would be nothing worth saving," Mr Peeler said.
"Our customers were walking in, they were walking around the premises, and they were  Summarize this news article:
broken.
He said the conversations afterwards were difficult, and the stories that followed were "still hard to deal with".
"You knew their livelihood was gone, their super was probably gone, their recovery вҖҰ that's going to be a long process," Mr Peeler said.
"There were young couples that had set up wineries, brought their wine to our premises, happily we stacked it away.
"They were nearly to the point of giving up their day jobs and starting something they worked all their life for.
Trevor Peeler says he finds it difficult to talk about the destruction of the coolstore.(ABC News: Danielle Bonica)
Rachel Vandstone, a volunteer at the donation centre in Harcourt, also became emotional towards the end of delivering 21 recommendations to the hearing, including upgrading the leisure centre.
"This place right here, this room, this is our community place, this is where we've been," she said.
"Invest in this place вҖҰ as a fit-for-purpose emergency and community hub.
"This building has served

australia.title: Hopes new Canberra strata recommendations will dispel 'shonks' from industry
summary: Summarize this news article:
A probe into strata management in Canberra has recommended those working in the industry become licensed and be forced to disclose conflicts of interest and commissions.

The ACT Legislative Assembly inquiry has also recommended the ACT government establish a strata commissioner, who could handle disputes before they are escalated to the ACT Civil and Administrative Tribunal.
The committee made 33 recommendations, with its report highlighting concerns raised by residents, including strata managers receiving "kick-backs" from insurers.

"I have noticed that a lot of insurance companies are very close and almost in bed with strata managers. They sponsor conferences вҖҰ There is strata manager of the year, which is sponsored by an insurance company," resident Helen Goddard told the committee.
"I feel very uncomfortable about those sorts of things," she added.
Anne Cahill Lambert and her partner downsized to an apartment complex six years ago that has had a number of building defects, includin Summarize this news article:
g incorrectly installed balustrades.
Anne Cahill Lambert says battling developers and strata managers over building defects has been frustrating.(ABC News: Simon Beardsell)

"We were bullied into letting these things go on the basis that 'they're all the same in this building'. We are idiots. Our experience over the last nearly five years highlights many shortcomings in relation to strata management in the ACT," she said.
Ms Cahill Lambert, who advocated for the inquiry and made a submission, said she hoped the recommendations would "really put some sunlight" on the issues in strata management.
She particularly welcomed the recommendation of a strata commissioner.
"It'll stop people from having to go down a legal route вҖҰ it'll bring some sense and concentration into what should be happening in strata land," Ms Cahill Lambert said.
An ACT government spokesperson said it was considering the recommendations and would respond in due course.

australia.title: School denies parents' claims of how AI deepfake scandal was handled
summary: Summarize this news article:

A Tasmanian private school criticised for its handling of an AI deepfake incident has denied it ever tried to discourage parents from telling their daughters they had been identified in sexually explicit images.

Two parents told the ABCthat senior staff at The Friends' School had called to tell them their daughters had been identified in AI-generated pornographic images and went on to advise them that most parents were not going to tell their children.
The faked images of the girls were allegedly shared in a group chat by male students. The parents said police told them 21 girls had been identified as victims.
Five boys have since left the school and police said no charges had been laid.

In a formal complaint to the school, one parent wrote: "I was advised [by the school] that the majority of parents had chosen not to inform their children that they had been identified, and that this was considered to be in the best interests of the students."
The two parents told the ABC their daughte Summarize this news article:
rs felt isolated and unsupported because they did not know if any of their peers had been told.

In an email to all school parents late on Wednesday night, the Friends' School principal, Esther Hill, said: "At no time have families been advised or encouraged to withhold information from their child, nor to prevent their child from speaking with trusted adults."
Principal Esther Hill says the school is balancing "transparency, the integrity of the investigation, and the wellbeing of those involved".(The Friends' School)

She said she was "concerned by aspects" of the ABC's reporting, "including omissions of important elements вҖ” such as the privacy and agency of individuals, the support provided to families, and the guidance of police and other authorities".
This reporting does not "accurately reflect the situation", she wrote.
The ABC included large parts of a statement from the school in its reporting and also spoke to police and support services.
One of the two parents who spoke to the

india.title: US to attack Iran again? 'Short and powerful' wave of strikes may be in store
summary: Summarize this news article:

NEW DELHI: The US Central Command has prepared plans for a ГўВҖВңshort and powerfulГўВҖВқ wave of strikes on Iran, likely targeting key infrastructure, according to an Axios report, as President Donald Trump is set to be briefed on fresh military options on Thursday.

The briefing, to be led by CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper, signals that Trump is weighing the possibility of resuming major combat operations, either to break the deadlock in ongoing negotiations or to deliver a decisive blow before ending the conflict.

According to the report, CENTCOM has drawn up a plan for a ГўВҖВңshort and powerfulГўВҖВқ wave of strikes on Iran, aimed at pushing Tehran back to the negotiating table with greater flexibility on the nuclear issue. Another option expected to be presented involves taking control of parts of the Strait of Hormuz to reopen it to commercial shipping, potentially involving the deployment of ground forces.
A further proposal under consideration includes a special forces operation to  Summarize this news article:
secure IranГўВҖВҷs stockpile of highly enriched uranium, the report said.
Trump told Axios on Wednesday that he viewed the ongoing naval blockade against Iran as ГўВҖВңsomewhat more effective than the bombing.ГўВҖВқ
Sources cited in the report said the US president currently sees the blockade as his primary leverage but remains open to military action if Iran does not yield.
US military planners are also assessing the possibility of Iranian retaliation against American forces in the region in response to the blockade.
Do you think military pressure would help in US-Iran nuclear negotiations?
Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff General Dan Caine is also expected to attend the briefing, sources said. The White House did not respond to requests for comment.

india.title: 24 hospitalised after chlorine gas leak at defunct Pune water purification plant
summary: Summarize this news article:
PUNE: Twenty-four persons hospitalised after chlorine gas leakage in Pune's Kondhwa area on Thursday, according to news agency PTI.
The leak originated from an abandoned tank containing chlorine at a defunct water purification plant godown in the Gangadham area around 1 am, according to officials.

"Following the leak and the subsequent spread of gas, residents in the vicinity complained of breathlessness. Upon receiving the call, fire teams were dispatched. Several citizens were evacuated, and the leaking tank was patched using safety equipment," a fire official said.
During the operation, 22 residents and two fire personnel complained of breathlessness and were rushed to the hospital, he said.
A man who previously operated the water purification plant at the site said he had dismantled the unit, but the tank containing chlorine appeared to have remained unattended and developed a leak.
What do you believe should be done to prevent further incidents like this?

india.title: Shardul Thakur: Impact sub without impact as Mumbai IndiansвҖҷ call raises questions
summary: Summarize this news article:

as an Impact Sub has triggered a wave of fan criticism after the all-rounder, who was introduced in the seventh over of the MI vs SRH fixture in IPL 2026, ended up not bowling a single delivery - despite MI bowlers enduring a torrid day with the ball at the Wankhede.
The move quickly became one of the biggest talking points from the match, with fans questioning the logic behind bringing in a frontline seam-bowling all-rounder and then not utilising him at all.
ГўВҖВҳWe havenГўВҖВҷt played good cricketГўВҖВҷ - Mahela Jayawardene after MIГўВҖВҷs biggest IPL loss vs CSK

After posting 243 on the board, MI were in for a rude shock as Abhishek Sharma and Travis Head raced to 115/0 in just seven overs. MI, who made a host of changes to their playing XI, decided to sub out wicketkeeper-batter Robin Minz in favour of Thakur, hoping to break the partnership and gain an extra bowling cushion.
But Thakur ended up not getting a bowl as SRH cantered home with a record chase with over an over to spare. On a day  Summarize this news article:
when every MI bowler was taken to the cleaners, it was a baffling move not to hand the ball to Thakur to stem the flow of runs from SRH batters.

Trent Boult conceded 41 runs in his four overs, while Jasprit Bumrah endured an unforgettable day, returning figures of 4-0-54-0. Allah Ghazanfar, despite picking up two wickets, also leaked runs, giving away 51 in his four overs, while Ashwani Kumar returned figures of 2-0-41-0.

Hardik Pandya went at an economy of 10.63, while Will JacksГўВҖВҷ solitary over cost 19 runs. Yet, there was no sign of Thakur, who has 113 wickets from 112 matches in the IPL and is generally known as a partnership-breaker.
HereГўВҖВҷs how social media reacted to the bizarre decision:
MI bowlers were taken apart by SRH batters, with none conceding less than 10 runs per over. Notably, the ever-reliable Jasprit Bumrah returned figures of 0/54 in four overs.
Will MI bounce back in the next match after this defeat?

india.title: Justice dept sues $5B tech firm for excluding Americans from applying to high-paying jobs
summary: Summarize this news article:

The US Department of Justice (DOJ) has sued California-based technology company Cloudera for allegedly discriminating against Americans from applying to high-paying technology jobs. The department claims that the company created a separate recruitment and hiring process to ГўВҖВңdeter US workers from applyingГўВҖВқ, adding that it ГўВҖВңalso did not consider them for lucrative technology jobs that the company earmarked for people with temporary employment visaГўВҖВқ. It further claimed that Cloudera created an email account that did not allow external emails, but still instructed applicants to use that unworkable email address to apply for jobs.

For those unaware, Cloudera is a data software company that was acquired by private equity firms KKR and Clayton, Dubilier & Rice in October 2021 for approximately $5.3 billion. It is no longer a publicly traded company.
HereГўВҖВҷs the official announcement made by DOJ suing Cloudera
